title: Update user experience for the date with time column
excerpt: When updating a date with time column, once the date is chosen the calendar popup closes, making it so you have to choose the time first, then the date. That’s fine, however, if you need to choose a date other than today…

posts.author: daniwhetstone (Danielle Whetstone)
posts.cookedHtml: When updating a date with time column, once the date is chosen the calendar popup closes, making it so you have to choose the time first, then the date. That’s fine, however, if you need to choose a date other than today, you choose the time, choose the date, but in choosing the date, it overrides the time entry to 9:00am and closes the calendar window, making it so you have to click on the date again to open the calendar and update the time. Very inefficient.

posts.author: Valeriia.H (Valeriia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there @daniwhetstone

Also for this you can try Time in Status app which track time the Item has spent in each workflow status. So you can monitor how fast the Item moves between statuses using app with reports.
You’ll get user-friendly data on the board in a few seconds.

Знімок екрана 2024-01-26 о 03.33.481920×488 50.2 KB

Also you will be able to track how much time your issue spends in each status and much more by generating 3 types of reports:

  • Time in Status Report
  • Status Count Report
  • Transition Count Report

And use charts to visualize your reports a sample of a certain number according to time range/item range, or a single item:

  • Pie Charts
  • Bar Charts
  • Area Charts

The app is also available in the format of board integration.

Знімок екрана 2024-01-26 о 03.35.011538×634 95.8 KB

You can try it on the Monday Marketplace and share your experience with us.
Hope you find this app helpful.
